name: bug_font_not_resolved_on_mount
description: A DOM that arrives by wholesale replacement (an e2e `mount`, an Update::RefreshDom, a route change) never has its font stacks re-resolved: the skip heuristic in layout/src/window.rs asked only 'is any node font-dirty?', and a brand-new StyledDom has no dirty nodes relative to itself, so font resolution was skipped ENTIRELY. A node with a font-family the window has never seen got no font loaded and silently rendered in the previous DOM's font — measured directly: eight distinct font families on screen left parsed_fonts / font_chain_cache / font_hash_to_families flat. This is the same 'derived state is never recomputed after a DOM swap' family as the stale-screen bug, and it is what made the font-leak scenario vacuously green (nothing is ever acquired, so 'it came back to baseline' is trivially true). The test asserts the contract directly: mounting text in a distinct family must GROW the font tables, and the text must actually paint.
